channel.title: general on Nithin Kamath's personal homepage
channel.description: Recent content in general on Nithin Kamath's personal homepage

item.title: The unease around wealth and inequality
item.description: I’ll admit this might sound odd coming from me, maybe even clichéd. But it’s something I’ve been sitting with for a while, so here goes. When I started out, like most people, I had a simple wealth goal. I’d actually written it down: hit ₹5 crore, retire in Goa, beach shack, done. That was the dream. After the Zerodha journey, I find myself on a very different side of that equation, and the dark inequalities of wealth and opportunity are harder to ignore than ever.

item.title: Intermission by The Ken, documenting the history of Indian businesses
item.description: India has a rich business history. Almost none of it has been documented. This apathy applies broadly, but especially to business, finance, and markets, and it’s something I’ve found genuinely frustrating for a long time. So I’m really happy that the team at The Ken decided to take this on. I’ve been bugging them about it for years. The way I consume content has changed a lot since AI. The internet is flooded with generated slop, and it’s made me much more deliberate.

item.title: Intermission by The Ken, documenting the history of Indian businesses
item.description: India has a rich business history. Almost none of it has been documented. This apathy applies broadly, but especially to business, finance, and markets, and it’s something I’ve found genuinely frustrating for a long time. So I’m really happy that the team at The Ken decided to take this on. I’ve been bugging them about it for years. The way I consume content has changed a lot since AI. The internet is flooded with generated slop, and it’s made me much more deliberate.

item.title: Fostering love for reading among children
item.description: Seema and I recently took part in India Reads Aloud, an initiative by Turning Pages to foster a love for reading in children. Reading is foundational to curiosity, but given the structure of our lives, it’s easier to hand kids devices. Once they’re on them, the kids lose themselves and can remain endlessly distracted. Even I, as an adult, have a hard time not falling into the trap of endlessly consuming digital content.

item.title: Civic sense starts with us
item.description: We often blame the government or other people for many societal issues, but real change begins with us. People watch, copy, and learn from what they see, whether it’s good or bad. Sadly, even the educated and elite rarely set the right examples when it comes to civic sense. This Zero1ByZerodha video explores how our lack of civic responsibility affects us all. It’s already hit nearly 1 million views in just 8 days.
